query: Stress-test top load-bearing risks in PROJECT.md v2 with current 2026 external sources
refinedQuery: Validate PROJECT.md §9 risk register entries — Tauri service update, SmartScreen/Defender, MS native parity, OSS conversion, Polish trademark — against current canonical sources

findings.claim: Tauri sidecar pattern does NOT fit external Windows services (lifecycle tied to UI). Two-process pattern is feasible (Firezone in prod) but auto-update of the service binary has no canonical Tauri pattern. PROJECT.md spike scope is correct; add named-pipe IPC + service stop/swap/restart to spike checklist.

Per MS Learn (2026-05-09): EV no longer bypasses SmartScreen. OV and EV treated identically. Reputation builds from 'several weeks and hundreds of clean installs.' PROJECT.md mitigation should switch from SignPath/Certum to Microsoft Artifact Signing (~$10/mo, no token, CI/CD-native).

Multiple CA marketing sites (DigiCert, Sectigo, SSL.com) still claim EV grants instant SmartScreen reputation. Microsoft canonical doc contradicts this explicitly. Trust Microsoft, ignore CA marketing.

wsl --manage <distro> --resize is shipped GA in WSL 2.5+. Auto-shrink-on-idle not yet shipped but is plausible 12-month roadmap given open issue #4699. PROJECT.md 12-24 month horizon for WSL wedge is too long — tighten to 6-18 months for WSL specifically; Storage Sense dev-cache integration is still 12-24 mo.

Industry benchmarks: 0.3-1% for mass-market dev tools, 1-3% enterprise OSS, 3%+ exceptional. PROJECT.md's 0.3-0.8% (stretch 1.0%) is well-calibrated. Structural LTV/CAC problem not solvable by conversion tweaks alone.

Surface search returned no exact 'POLISH' software-class Class 9/42 mark. Many polish-related marks exist (mostly nail polish). Inconclusive — direct USPTO TESS + EUIPO eSearch Plus check required pre-launch. PROJECT.md risk rating High/High appropriate.

Win11 Smart App Control blocks execution of unsigned/low-rep files at runtime, not just download. Applies to all executable files including service binaries post-install. Default-on for clean Win11 installs since 22H2. No user 'Run anyway' override on SAC-enabled machines. PROJECT.md §9 missing this risk entirely.

keyInsights: Microsoft now treats OV and EV code signing certificates identically for SmartScreen — PROJECT.md mitigation should swap to Artifact Signing (~$10/mo, CI/CD-native, no hardware token)
WSL --manage --resize is shipped GA — Polish's WSL Dev wedge has a 6-18 month window, not 12-24
Smart App Control on Win11 is a new and missing risk in PROJECT.md §9 — blocks low-rep binaries at execution, not just download
Tauri's standard sidecar pattern does not fit Windows-service architecture; the Week 1-3 spike PROJECT.md plans is the correct response
OSS free-flagship conversion benchmarks (0.3-1%) confirm PROJECT.md's honest projections — Pro tier LTV/CAC is structurally broken unless feature expansion or higher ACV
redFlags: PROJECT.md signing strategy (SignPath Foundation 4-8 week wait + Certum fallback) is outdated — Microsoft Artifact Signing supersedes both for this use case
PROJECT.md MS native parity 12-24 month assumption too lax for WSL specifically
Smart App Control missing from risk register entirely
CA vendor marketing copy on EV certificates is misleading; do not rely on it for SmartScreen planning
gaps: No quantitative data on Defender PUP/HackTool false-positive rates for cleanup utilities
No direct USPTO TESS / EUIPO Class 9/42 'POLISH' authoritative check (web search insufficient)
No published install-completion delta for new-cert binaries on SAC-enabled Win11
No canonical Tauri 2 + Windows-service auto-update example or sample repo
WSL 2.5 --manage --resize adoption rate among dev population unknown — proxy for wedge erosion
